tracked.name: Resting Heart Rate
tracked.desc: The number of times your heart beats per minute while at complete rest.

tracked.name: Systolic Blood Pressure
tracked.range: <120 mmHg (normal), 120–129 mmHg (elevated), 130–139 mmHg (Stage 1 HTN), ≥140 mmHg (Stage 2 HTN)
tracked.desc: The pressure exerted on artery walls when the heart contracts and pumps blood.

tracked.name: Diastolic Blood Pressure
tracked.range: <80 mmHg (normal), 80–89 mmHg (Stage 1 HTN), ≥90 mmHg (Stage 2 HTN)
tracked.desc: The pressure in arteries when the heart relaxes between beats.

tracked.name: Blood Pressure Trend
tracked.range: Average home BP <135/85 mmHg; average 24-hour ABPM <130/80 mmHg
tracked.desc: A longitudinal analysis of blood pressure readings over time to identify patterns such as sustained hypertension, white-coat effect, masked hypertension, or nocturnal dipping patterns.

tracked.name: Blood Oxygen Saturation
tracked.desc: The percentage of hemoglobin binding sites occupied by oxygen in arterial blood.

tracked.name: Body Temperature
tracked.range: 97.8–99.1°F (36.6–37.3°C) orally; varies with time of day and individual
tracked.desc: Core body temperature reflects the balance between heat production (metabolism, muscular activity) and heat loss.

tracked.name: Hemoglobin
tracked.range: Men: 13.5–17.5 g/dL; Women: 12.0–16.0 g/dL; Pregnant women: 11.0–14.0 g/dL
tracked.desc: The iron-containing protein in red blood cells responsible for carrying oxygen from the lungs to tissues and returning carbon dioxide.

tracked.name: White Blood Cell Count
tracked.desc: The total number of white blood cells in the blood, reflecting the body's immune defense capacity.

tracked.name: Platelet Count
tracked.desc: The number of platelets (thrombocytes) in the blood.

tracked.name: Fasting Blood Glucose (Self-Monitored)
tracked.range: Normal: 70–99 mg/dL; Prediabetes: 100–125 mg/dL; Diabetes: ≥126 mg/dL (confirmed on two occasions)
tracked.desc: Blood sugar level measured after at least 8 hours of fasting using a home glucometer.

tracked.name: Hemoglobin A1c
tracked.range: Normal: <5.7%; Prediabetes: 5.7–6.4%; Diabetes: ≥6.5%
tracked.desc: The percentage of hemoglobin that has glucose attached, reflecting average blood glucose levels over the preceding 2–3 months.

tracked.name: Fasting Plasma Glucose (Lab)
tracked.range: Normal: 70–99 mg/dL; Impaired Fasting Glucose (prediabetes): 100–125 mg/dL; Diabetes: ≥126 mg/dL
tracked.desc: A laboratory measurement of plasma glucose after a minimum 8-hour overnight fast.

tracked.name: Thyroid Stimulating Hormone
tracked.range: 0.4–4.0 mIU/L (some experts advocate upper limit of 2.5 mIU/L)
tracked.desc: A pituitary hormone that regulates thyroid gland function.

tracked.name: TSH Monitoring (On Treatment)
tracked.range: Target TSH: 0.5–2.5 mIU/L for most patients on levothyroxine; 0.1–0.5 for thyroid cancer suppression therapy
tracked.desc: Regular TSH monitoring for patients on thyroid hormone replacement or antithyroid medications.

tracked.name: Total Cholesterol
tracked.range: Desirable: <200 mg/dL; Borderline high: 200–239 mg/dL; High: ≥240 mg/dL
tracked.desc: The sum of all cholesterol carried in lipoproteins — LDL, HDL, VLDL, and others.

tracked.name: LDL Cholesterol
tracked.range: Optimal: <100 mg/dL; Near optimal: 100–129; Borderline high: 130–159; High: 160–189; Very high: ≥190 mg/dL
tracked.desc: Low-density lipoprotein cholesterol is the primary driver of atherosclerotic plaque formation.

tracked.name: HDL Cholesterol
tracked.range: Men: >40 mg/dL; Women: >50 mg/dL; Optimal: >60 mg/dL
tracked.desc: High-density lipoprotein cholesterol participates in reverse cholesterol transport — moving cholesterol from arterial walls back to the liver for excretion.

tracked.name: Triglycerides
tracked.range: Normal: <150 mg/dL; Borderline: 150–199; High: 200–499; Very high: ≥500 mg/dL
tracked.desc: The main form of fat in the blood, carried in VLDL and chylomicrons.

tracked.name: Lipid Panel Monitoring (On Treatment)
tracked.range: On high-intensity statin: expect ≥50% LDL reduction. On moderate-intensity: 30–49% reduction. Specific targets depend on risk category.
tracked.desc: Regular lipid panel monitoring for patients on lipid-lowering therapy (statins, ezetimibe, PCSK9 inhibitors, fibrates).

tracked.name: Alanine Aminotransferase
tracked.range: Men: 7–56 U/L; Women: 7–45 U/L (updated guidelines suggest upper normal: 33 U/L men, 25 U/L women)
tracked.desc: A liver enzyme released into the blood when hepatocytes (liver cells) are damaged.

tracked.name: Creatinine / eGFR
tracked.range: Creatinine: Men 0.7–1.3 mg/dL, Women 0.6–1.1 mg/dL; eGFR: >90 mL/min/1.73m² (normal), 60–89 (mildly decreased), 30–59 (moderately decreased), 15–29 (severely decreased), <15 (kidney failure)
tracked.desc: Serum creatinine is a waste product from muscle metabolism filtered by the kidneys.

tracked.name: Urinalysis
tracked.range: pH: 4.5–8.0; Specific gravity: 1.005–1.030; Protein: negative; Glucose: negative; Blood: negative; WBC: 0–5/HPF; RBC: 0–3/HPF; No casts, bacteria, or crystals
tracked.desc: A comprehensive urine screening test evaluating physical (color, clarity, specific gravity), chemical (pH, protein, glucose, blood, leukocyte esterase, nitrites, ketones, bilirubin), and microscopic (cells, casts, crystals, bacteria) properties.

tracked.name: Sleep Duration
tracked.range: 7–9 hours (ages 18–64); 7–8 hours (ages 65+)
tracked.desc: The total amount of sleep obtained per 24-hour period.

tracked.name: Mood & Stress Level
tracked.range: PSS-10 score 0–13 (low stress); PHQ-9 score 0–4 (minimal depression); GAD-7 score 0–4 (minimal anxiety)
tracked.desc: A self-reported assessment of psychological well-being, emotional state, and perceived stress burden.

tracked.name: Cortisol
tracked.range: Morning (6–8 AM): 10–20 mcg/dL (276–552 nmol/L); Evening: 3–10 mcg/dL
tracked.desc: The primary glucocorticoid stress hormone produced by the adrenal cortex.

tracked.name: Cognitive Function Score
tracked.range: MoCA ≥26/30 (normal); MMSE ≥24/30 (normal)
tracked.desc: A standardized assessment of cognitive abilities including memory, attention, executive function, language, and visuospatial skills.

tracked.name: Reaction Time
tracked.range: Simple reaction time: 150–300 ms (visual), 120–180 ms (auditory). Choice reaction time: 300–500 ms.
tracked.desc: The time elapsed between presentation of a stimulus and the initiation of a motor response.

tracked.name: C-Reactive Protein
tracked.range: hs-CRP <1.0 mg/L (low cardiovascular risk); 1.0–3.0 mg/L (moderate risk); >3.0 mg/L (high risk). Standard CRP <10 mg/L.
tracked.desc: An acute-phase protein produced by the liver in response to inflammation.

tracked.name: Erythrocyte Sedimentation Rate
tracked.range: Men: 0–15 mm/hr (age <50), 0–20 mm/hr (age >50). Women: 0–20 mm/hr (age <50), 0–30 mm/hr (age >50).
tracked.desc: A nonspecific marker of inflammation that measures how quickly red blood cells settle to the bottom of a test tube in one hour.

tracked.name: Immunoglobulin G (Total)
tracked.desc: The most abundant immunoglobulin in serum, comprising 70–80% of total antibodies.

tracked.name: Immunoglobulin A (Total)
tracked.range: Serum IgA: 70–400 mg/dL (0.7–4.0 g/L). Salivary IgA: 80–350 µg/mL.
tracked.desc: The predominant antibody in mucosal surfaces (gut, respiratory tract, saliva, tears).

tracked.name: Total IgE
tracked.range: Total IgE: <100 IU/mL (adults). Varies widely; atopic individuals may have 200–2,000+ IU/mL.
tracked.desc: Immunoglobulin E is the antibody class responsible for allergic reactions and defense against parasitic infections.

tracked.name: Vitamin A (Retinol)
tracked.desc: A fat-soluble vitamin essential for vision, immune function, cell differentiation, and reproduction.

tracked.name: Vitamin B1 (Thiamine)
tracked.range: Whole blood thiamine: 70–180 nmol/L. Erythrocyte TPP: 120–150 nmol/L.
tracked.desc: A water-soluble vitamin essential for carbohydrate metabolism and neural function.

tracked.name: Vitamin B2 (Riboflavin)
tracked.range: EGRAC <1.2 (adequate); 1.2–1.4 (marginal); >1.4 (deficient). Serum riboflavin: 6–24 µg/L.
tracked.desc: A water-soluble vitamin that serves as a precursor to the coenzymes flavin mononucleotide (FMN) and flavin adenine dinucleotide (FAD).

tracked.name: Vitamin B3 (Niacin)
tracked.range: Urinary N1-methylnicotinamide: 5.8–17.5 µmol/day. No well-established serum reference range.
tracked.desc: A water-soluble vitamin that is a precursor to NAD+ and NADP+, coenzymes involved in over 400 enzymatic reactions including energy metabolism, DNA repair, and cell signaling.

tracked.name: Vitamin B5 (Pantothenic Acid)
tracked.range: Whole blood: 1.6–2.7 µmol/L. Urinary excretion: 2–7 mg/day.
tracked.desc: A water-soluble vitamin that is a component of coenzyme A (CoA) and acyl carrier protein, both essential for fatty acid synthesis and oxidation, citric acid cycle, and the synthesis of steroid hormones, neurotransmitters, and hemoglobin.

tracked.name: Vitamin B6 (Pyridoxine)
tracked.range: Plasma PLP: 20–200 nmol/L (5–50 µg/L)
tracked.desc: A water-soluble vitamin with six vitamer forms, of which pyridoxal 5'-phosphate (PLP) is the active coenzyme.

tracked.name: Vitamin B7 (Biotin)
tracked.range: Serum biotin: 200–1,200 pg/mL (0.8–4.9 nmol/L)
tracked.desc: A water-soluble vitamin that serves as a coenzyme for five carboxylase enzymes involved in gluconeogenesis, fatty acid synthesis, and amino acid metabolism.

tracked.name: Folate (Vitamin B9)
tracked.range: Serum folate: 7–45 nmol/L (3–20 ng/mL). RBC folate: 340–1,020 nmol/L (150–450 ng/mL).
tracked.desc: A water-soluble B vitamin essential for one-carbon metabolism, DNA synthesis, repair, and methylation.

tracked.name: Vitamin B12 (Cobalamin)
tracked.range: Serum B12: 200–900 pg/mL (148–664 pmol/L). MMA: <370 nmol/L.
tracked.desc: A water-soluble vitamin essential for DNA synthesis, methylation, neurological function, and red blood cell formation.

tracked.name: Vitamin C (Ascorbic Acid)
tracked.range: Plasma: 0.6–2.0 mg/dL (34–114 µmol/L). Deficiency: <0.2 mg/dL (<11 µmol/L).
tracked.desc: A water-soluble vitamin and potent antioxidant essential for collagen synthesis, carnitine biosynthesis, neurotransmitter production, and immune function.

tracked.name: Vitamin D (25-Hydroxyvitamin D)
tracked.range: Sufficient: 30–100 ng/mL (75–250 nmol/L). Insufficient: 20–29 ng/mL. Deficient: <20 ng/mL (<50 nmol/L).
tracked.desc: A fat-soluble prohormone essential for calcium absorption, bone mineralization, immune modulation, and muscle function.

tracked.name: Vitamin E (Alpha-Tocopherol)
tracked.range: 5–20 mg/L (12–46 µmol/L). Lipid-adjusted ratio: >0.8 mg/g total lipids.
tracked.desc: A fat-soluble antioxidant that protects cell membranes from lipid peroxidation.

tracked.name: Vitamin K
tracked.range: Serum phylloquinone: 0.2–3.2 nmol/L (0.1–1.4 ng/mL). PT/INR normal: 11–13.5 sec / 0.9–1.1.
tracked.desc: A fat-soluble vitamin essential for blood coagulation and bone metabolism.

tracked.name: Ferritin
tracked.range: Men: 30–300 ng/mL. Women (premenopausal): 10–200 ng/mL. Women (postmenopausal): 30–300 ng/mL.
tracked.desc: The primary intracellular iron storage protein.

tracked.name: Total Iron-Binding Capacity
tracked.desc: A measure of the blood's capacity to bind iron with transferrin, the primary iron transport protein.

tracked.name: Transferrin Saturation
tracked.desc: The percentage of transferrin binding sites occupied by iron, calculated as (serum iron / TIBC) × 100.

tracked.name: Zinc
tracked.desc: An essential trace mineral involved in over 300 enzymatic reactions, including DNA synthesis, immune function, wound healing, protein synthesis, and taste/smell perception.

tracked.name: Selenium
tracked.desc: An essential trace mineral incorporated as selenocysteine into 25 selenoproteins including glutathione peroxidases (antioxidant defense), thioredoxin reductases, and iodothyronine deiodinases (thyroid hormone conversion).

tracked.name: Copper
tracked.range: Serum copper: 70–140 µg/dL (11–22 µmol/L). Ceruloplasmin: 20–35 mg/dL.
tracked.desc: An essential trace mineral required for iron metabolism (ceruloplasmin), connective tissue formation (lysyl oxidase), energy production (cytochrome c oxidase), antioxidant defense (Cu/Zn-SOD), and neurotransmitter synthesis (dopamine beta-hydroxylase).

tracked.name: Iodine
tracked.range: Median UIC: 100–199 µg/L (adequate). 50–99 µg/L (mild deficiency). <50 µg/L (moderate deficiency). <20 µg/L (severe).
tracked.desc: An essential trace mineral required exclusively for thyroid hormone synthesis (T3 and T4).

tracked.name: RBC Magnesium
tracked.range: RBC Mg: 4.2–6.8 mg/dL (1.7–2.8 mmol/L). Compare with serum Mg: 1.7–2.2 mg/dL.
tracked.desc: Intracellular magnesium status measured in red blood cells, which better reflects tissue magnesium status than serum levels.

tracked.name: Omega-3 Index
tracked.range: Desirable: ≥8%. Intermediate: 4–8%. High risk: <4%.
tracked.desc: The percentage of EPA (eicosapentaenoic acid) plus DHA (docosahexaenoic acid) in red blood cell membranes.

tracked.name: Prealbumin (Transthyretin)
tracked.desc: A hepatic transport protein with a short half-life (2–3 days) that makes it a sensitive marker of recent nutritional status and protein-calorie intake.

tracked.name: Body Mass Index
tracked.range: Underweight: <18.5. Normal: 18.5–24.9. Overweight: 25.0–29.9. Obese I: 30.0–34.9. Obese II: 35.0–39.9. Obese III: ≥40.0 kg/m².
tracked.desc: A simple index of weight-for-height calculated as weight (kg) divided by height squared (m²).

tracked.name: Body Fat Percentage
tracked.range: Men: Essential fat 2–5%, Athletes 6–13%, Fitness 14–17%, Acceptable 18–24%, Obese >25%. Women: Essential fat 10–13%, Athletes 14–20%, Fitness 21–24%, Acceptable 25–31%, Obese >32%.
tracked.desc: The proportion of total body mass that is adipose tissue.

tracked.name: Waist Circumference
tracked.range: Men: <94 cm (<37 inches) normal, 94–102 cm increased risk, >102 cm (>40 inches) substantially increased risk. Women: <80 cm (<31.5 inches) normal, 80–88 cm increased risk, >88 cm (>35 inches) substantially increased risk.
tracked.desc: The circumference of the abdomen measured at the midpoint between the lower rib margin and the iliac crest.

tracked.name: Lean Body Mass
tracked.range: Varies by sex, height, and age. Appendicular skeletal muscle mass index (ASMI = appendicular lean mass / height²): Men: >7.0 kg/m². Women: >5.5 kg/m².
tracked.desc: Total body weight minus fat mass, comprising muscle, bone, water, organs, and connective tissue.

tracked.name: Visceral Fat Area
tracked.range: CT/MRI: VFA <100 cm² (low risk), 100–130 cm² (moderate), >130 cm² (high risk). BIA visceral fat level: 1–9 (normal), 10–14 (high), ≥15 (very high).
tracked.desc: Fat stored around abdominal organs (liver, intestines, pancreas) that is metabolically active and pathogenic.

tracked.name: Waist-to-Hip Ratio
tracked.range: Men: <0.90 (low risk), 0.90–0.99 (moderate), ≥1.0 (high risk). Women: <0.80 (low risk), 0.80–0.85 (moderate), >0.85 (high risk).
tracked.desc: The ratio of waist circumference to hip circumference, indicating body fat distribution.

tracked.name: Bone Mineral Content
tracked.range: Total body BMC: Men 2,500–3,500 g, Women 2,000–2,800 g. Varies with body size and ethnicity.
tracked.desc: The total amount of mineral (primarily calcium hydroxyapatite) in the skeleton, measured in grams.

tracked.name: Total Body Water
tracked.range: Men: 55–65% of body weight. Women: 45–55% of body weight.
tracked.desc: The total volume of water in the body, comprising intracellular fluid (ICF, ~60% of TBW) and extracellular fluid (ECF, ~40%).

tracked.name: Menstrual Cycle Tracking
tracked.range: Cycle length: 21–35 days; Flow duration: 3–7 days; Blood loss: 5–80 mL per cycle (>80 mL = heavy menstrual bleeding)
tracked.desc: Menstrual cycle tracking monitors cycle length, regularity, flow duration, and associated symptoms as vital indicators of reproductive and overall health.
